title: Board Member Authority
description: Individual school board members have authority only when acting as part of the full board — no single member can make decisions, give directives, or speak officially on behalf of the district alone. This means parents and community members should understand that requests or statements from one board member do not carry official weight unless the whole board has voted on the matter. Knowing this helps the community understand how decisions are actually made and who is authorized to act on behalf of Conroe ISD.

timeline.summary: This is Conroe ISD's local policy BBE governing Board Member authority. The policy establishes that the Board has final authority over district policies, that individual board members only have authority when acting collectively as a board in a duly called meeting or when delegated specific authority by Board action. The document shows one addition (in red/underlined text) clarifying the scope of individual board member authority.
changes_added: An individual Board member has authority only when acting as a member of the Board in a duly called meeting or when delegated specific authority by Board action.

### BBE Local — Board Members Authority
- Discussion about individual board member authority vs. collective board action.
- General counsel clarified this prevents individual board members from taking action or speaking on behalf of the board without authorization.
- Discussion about delegation of authority to the board president (e.g., setting the agenda, speaking to media).
